hero_subtitle: SFTY helps compliance teams document and trace on-chain suspicious activity by linking wallet risk, transaction alerts, and multi-provider data into audit-ready cases and reports.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Suspicious activity evidence becomes harder to trace
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to preserve and link suspicious transaction evidence across multiple KYT providers and workflows. This fragmentation slows reviews, weakens audit readiness, and complicates defensible suspicious activity reporting for VASPs and digital asset businesses.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects transaction alerts, wallet risk data and provider outputs into structured cases with complete audit trails so compliance teams can trace and preserve suspicious activity evidence efficiently.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY collects wallet risk signals and transaction alerts to open cases, support review, preserve evidence and prepare reports for suspicious activity workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond wallet alerts. One evidence layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects multi-provider KYT outputs into structured cases, decisions and evidence to support operational control, audit readiness and transparent suspicious activity review.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with controlled workflows to preserve audit-ready evidence and provider outputs essential for bank due diligence and enterprise compliance deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for suspicious activity review?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects wallet risk, transaction alerts and regulatory triggers into structured cases for suspicious activity review and reporting, providing a connected compliance workflow for detecting and managing digital asset risks.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle KYT alert workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes KYT alerts into configurable review workflows, linking alerts to customers, wallets, transactions and cases with decision logs and escalation paths tailored for suspicious activity management.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve suspicious activity evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, supporting evidence, decision records and audit logs to support suspicious activity reviews are defensible and support reporting workflows obligations.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing KYT providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above KYT providers, aggregating their outputs into a single operational layer without requiring replacement or direct modification of existing provider integrations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC, KYT or other providers but connects their outputs into structured, auditable compliance workflows that reduce manual fragmentation and improve decision traceability.
final_title: Preserve suspicious activity evidence with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects wallet risk, transaction alerts and provider data into controlled, audit-ready cases and compliance workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
